items.title: Maak kennis met FC Noah, de bijbelse uitdager van AZ: 'Het leek wel een film'
items.summary: In de tussenronde van de Conference League gaat AZ donderdag op bezoek bij FC Noah. Een club met een bijbelse naam, geworteld in een land dat zich losrukt van de Russische invloedssfeer en voorzichtig toenadering zoekt tot het Westen. Wat schuilt er achter deze onbekende opponent uit Armenië? Tijd voor een nadere kennismaking met FC Noah, dat balanceert tussen de Sovjet-erfenis en de digitale toekomst.

items.title: Engelse clubs beleven op één na beste Champions League-seizoen ooit
items.summary: De uithaal tegen Qarabag onderstreepte niet alleen het machtsvertoon van Newcastle United, maar ook de dominantie van de Premier League op het Europese podium. Voor het eerst in de geschiedenis van de Champions League gaan zes ploegen uit hetzelfde land zich voor de knock-outfase plaatsen.

items.title: Poku mee naar het WK? 'Het is een specialist'
items.summary: Ernest Poku ontwikkelt zich sterk bij Bayer Leverkusen en was tegen Olympiakos verantwoordelijk voor een assist in de met 0-2 gewonnen wedstrijd. Aan tafel bij Rondje Europa wordt hardop nagedacht of Ronald Koeman ook moet denken aan de rappe buitenspeler, die een wapen zou kunnen zijn op het aankomende WK.

items.title: Vinícius Jr. racism allegation prompts UEFA probe
items.summary: UEFA will investigate the racism allegations made by Vinícius Júnior in Real Madrid's game against Benfica.

items.title: Benfica: 'Smear campaign' after Viní allegations
items.summary: SL Benfica have condemned the "smear campaign" to which their player Gianluca Prestianni has been subjected to following allegations that he racially abused Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal 'didn't show anything' vs. Wolves
items.summary: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta said his team have only themselves to blame after surrendering a two-goal lead to draw 2-2 with rock-bottom Wolves at Molineux, adding that all criticism of his side was fair after the capitulation.

items.title: Bodo/Glimt add win over Inter to impressive run
items.summary: Champions League runner-up Inter Milan became the latest top team to suffer an upset by Bodø/Glimt in the Champions League, losing 3-1 away in the first leg of their playoff on Wednesday.

items.title: Zo zijn de AZ'ers tot nu toe beoordeeld in de Conference League
items.summary: Kees Smit is vooralsnog de best beoordeelde AZ-speler in de Conference League. De middenvelder noteerde een moyenne van 6,90 in de competitiefase.

items.title: Weer bizarre belofte in strijd om voorzittershamer Barcelona
items.summary: Een van de kandidaten om de nieuwe voorzitter van Barcelona te worden, Xavier Vilajoana, heeft zich ambitieus getoond op sociale media.

items.title: How Szoboszlai became Liverpool's key man
items.summary: Inside the Hungarian midfielder's season as the main character at Liverpool, his friendship with Mohamed Salah and why the Reds are keen to extend his contract.

items.title: Leicester appeal against six-point deduction
items.summary: Leicester City appeal against a decision to deduct them six points for breaching English Football League financial rules.

items.title: Leicester and Premier League appeal over club’s six-point deduction
items.summary: Club unhappy sanction applied in Championship Premier League seeking additional punishment Leicester City’s legal battle with the Premier League continues to rumble on as both parties formally lodged appeals related to this month’s decision by an independent commission to deduct the club six points. It is understood the league believes Leicester should be punished additionally for the late submission of their annual accounts for 2023-24, to avoid setting a precedent, and said an appeal board will “urgently” hear the case to ensure it is resolved before the end of the English Football League season in May. Continue reading...

items.title: Alexander Isak should return from injury for Liverpool in April, says Arne Slot
items.summary: £125m summer signing broke a leg in December He should rejoin team training late March or early April Arne Slot is expecting Alexander Isak to be available for a Liverpool return in April. The striker fractured his left leg and sustained an ankle injury against Tottenham in December and required surgery but is back in light training. Isak started running this week as part of his rehabilitation and if he continues on the same path, he will join team training at the end of March. “It will be around that period of time – end of March, start of April – where he is hopefully back with the group,” the head coach said. Continue reading...

items.summary: “We have a lot of injured players, so we need to first find the best system that suits the players that are available at this moment, so a very particular moment.”

items.summary: Pep Guardiola is using new tactics this season by making Manchester City's attack more narrow, but did he hint at the idea 20 years ago?

items.title: 'Bottle word will be used' as Arsenal blow title advantage
items.summary: After blowing a two-goal lead to draw against the Premier League's bottom club Wolves, there will be no dodging the questions on whether Arsenal are mentally ready to end their 22-year wait to become champions.

items.title: Arteta accepts Arsenal ‘have to blame ourselves’ after blowing lead at Wolves
items.summary: League leaders squander 2-0 lead at bottom side Arteta: ‘We didn’t perform at the level required’ Mikel Arteta admitted Arsenal “have to blame ourselves” after they surrendered a two-goal lead to draw at Wolves. A 94th-minute equaliser cost the Premier League leaders two points as they allowed their rock-bottom opponents back into the game, further calling into question their title credentials. Arsenal are five points clear of Manchester City but have played a game more. The Gunners looked on their way to victory after goals from Bukayo Saka and Piero Hincapié, only to be pegged back by Hugo Bueno and Tom Edozie. Continue reading...
